issue_title: Add a "readinglist" prefix for statsd metrics
issue_body: Currently, we send information to statsd using `storage.{something}` or `cache.{something}`, and as such we're missing the `readinglist` prefix.

Along the ``storage`` and ``cache``, there would be a ``attachments`` backend.

Uses-cases are:
* Store big/files attached to your fxa account
* Add metadata to files via the related record (tags, ...)
* Query files using records API (filters, ...)

In the first iteration it should only:
* allow to add/remove attachments on records
* provide an internal api to allow more providers
* persist files on the filesystem

Mimic of couchdb attachments sounds like a good start https://wiki.apache.org/couchdb/HTTP_Document_API#Attachments

In the long term, ideally it should:
* allow *(e.g. encrypted)* chunk upload (aka resumable)
* allow Range (partial) downloads
* support SFTP, box.net, dropbox, gdrive, ...

* https://blogs.dropbox.com/developers/2012/08/chunked-uploads-beta/
* https://developers.google.com/drive/web/manage-uploads#resumable
* https://github.com/23/resumable.js
* https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.8/ref/files/storage/
* https://django-storages.readthedocs.org/en/latest/

issue_title: Switch from Last-Modified to ETag headers?
issue_body:
I think your `Last-Modified` headers are violating [RFC7232](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7232#section-2.2). According to this RFC, you need to use that long human-readable date format, chosen in a time when 1000 milliseconds was still a reasonable versioning granularity. :)

This means compliant http clients would consider your `Last-Modified` headers to be malformed, possibly leading to unpredictable behavior.

It is also common for a browser or other http client (or any proxy or CDN along the way) to insert its own `If-Modified-Since` header whenever it has a version of the document in cache, and there is no telling how a server implementation would react to that.

Also, violating RFCs is naughty. ;)

GoogleDrive, Dropbox, ownCloud-flavoured WebDAV, CouchDB, and remoteStorage all use ETag instead of Last-Modified. We used to have Last-Modified millisecond-timestamps in the remoteStorage protocol as well, but switched to ETags, because they do effectively the same thing, but are treated as opaque strings by http clients.

To make requests fail if a pre-existing resource changed:
* `If-Unmodified-Since: 1234567890123` -> `If-Match: "1234567890123"`

To make requests fail if a pre-existing resource did not change:
* `If-Modified-Since: 1234567890123` -> `If-None-Match: "1234567890123"`

To make requests fail if a presumed-absent resource exists:
* `If-Modified-Since: 0` -> `If-None-Match: *`

In http responses:
* `Last-Modified: 1234567890123` -> `ETag: "1234567890123"`

The downsides:
* Even when retrieving a resource for the first time, `If-Modified-Since` headers can be used to only retrieve a resource if it is recent, and `If-Unmodified-Since` to only retrieve it if it has stabilized. When using ETags, a client would have to do a filtered query on the collection first, and then decide whether to retrieve the resource or not. So you may have an extra roundtrip there, but I think this is not a very common case.
*  If you know the `Last-Modified` timestamp of a collection, then you could in theory forget the modification times of individual documents, and save some representation space. With ETags, the client needs to remember the ETag of each resource as metadata. But I think most client implementations would already do that anyway.

issue_title: Add a endpoint to delete all user data for a resource.
issue_body:
We should add an endpoint to delete all data associated to a user (for a specific resource).

cc @st3fan @rnewman.

I believe we should get rid of the code that disables the DELETE endpoint on collections via a specific setting.

IHMO, this should be : 
* out of cliquet scope (eg. do in readinglist endpoint override)
* applied to any endpoints through generic configuration (setting like ``{method}_{collection|record}_{resource}_enabled``)
* through options during registering of resource (see #12)

issue_title: Don't hmac userids with a server secret
issue_body:
Currently, [userids are hmaced with a secret only known by the server](https://github.com/mozilla-services/cliquet/blob/26f48ef35237e07efe1ea51b8d81b17c03e2b0a8/cliquet/authentication.py#L31).

This prevents the ability to replicate the permissions from one server to another, since the user_id would differ in these different instances.

Are we forced to hash the `user_id`, and if yes, why?

Refreshing the page makes it go away. Any hint of what might be the problem?

### Problem

In the Holding Pen interface, users are able to look at and interact with all objects running in workflows. For example, one object may have been halted in the workflow and flagged for approval by human (which can be then scheduled to continue through the `workflows.actions` interface "widgets").

1. How do we control access to this interface and the ability to "read" or "write" (e.g. see an object or be able to interact with it)? Shall we limit individual objects to users as per a few customizable parameters? Per workflow? Per "type"? Per "whatever the model has"? Or what about state (error, waiting, need action etc.) - presumably only admins are interested in programming exceptions being triggered in a workflow? Also, should access to an object change during the workflow?

2. How is access control managed when *running* workflows (from any source). Should the workflow be tied to a user or role?

### "Current" state

1. The first iteration of Holding Pen access control (seen on INSPIRE Labs) is just a simple gatekeeper: gain access or not to the interface. This is controlled via "viewholdingpen" authorization using the `invenio.ext.principal` extension that hooks into the `access` module (see WIP #2325). A user can then be connected to this action and thus access *anything* in Holding Pen. If user is not connected to this action (or a role connected to it) no access is granted.

2. Nothing really exists at the moment. A `id_user` column does exist in both the Workflow engine model and the Workflow object model, but it is mostly ignored/unused atm. I believe it was meant to signify the "owner".

### Proposal

1. For the interface, there could be two different types of authentication levels, read and write, at the workflow object (`BibWorkflowObject`) level and the workflow engine level (`BibWorkflowEngine`):
  >**read:** access to seeing (and filtering/searching) through any given record
  >**write:** access to manipulating (editing it, re-starting/continue workflow execution) any given record

2. For the "back-end" part, use the "write" access from above to control workflow execution.

The access can then be further managed through use of parameters to allow authorization based on the object/workflow properties.

*Silly question: Do we need the read/write distinction? Is it useful to allow seeing the records in Holding Pen but not touching - because why else be in Holding Pen if you are not there to "manipulate" things?*

#### Use cases:

Imagine a use-case where some user/role only should be able to access (and manipulate) submissions from workflow `my_submission`, then a new `runworkflows` authorization can be created with parameter `workflow.name == "my_submission"`and the user would then be allowed to see (and touch) objects in Holding Pen from this workflow.

Another case could be where access to an object is changed during a workflow. To allow this to change dynamically, we could use a new object property `roles/authorizations/restrictions etc.` or re-use the `extra_data` property, that can be modified in a task and predefined authorizations will look for it.

issue_title: xmlmarc2textmarc: do not modify record passed to create_marc_record()
issue_body:
Originally on 2012-07-09

When using the `create_marc_record()` facility, the signature is `create_marc_record(record, sysno, options)` and the returned value is a MARC record.

That function modifies the record passed, e.g 
```
del record["001"]
```
which is not desired, it should just output the textmarc.

In https://bugs.launchpad.net/zope.configuration/+bug/162166, @malthe reported:

> In `zope/configuration/config.py:register` it seems that it isn't being asserted that no previous registration of a directive with the same name has happened.
>
> This might be desirable (not sure why) and currently happens for these directives:
```python
('http://namespaces.zope.org/meta', u'directive')
('http://namespaces.zope.org/meta', u'groupingDirective')
('http://namespaces.zope.org/meta', u'complexDirective')
('http://namespaces.zope.org/zope', u'securityPolicy')
```
> So for instance, if Five registers the `<browser:viewlet>` then there's nothing to prevent another package from including zope.viewlet's meta.zcml which will then override that directive, breaking viewlet support in Zope 2.

The variable `OMP_NUM_THREADS` is somewhat the gold standard to configure the thread count of an application. Similar to tools like OpenBlas, numexpr should support this variable as a fallback method to its custom variable `NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS`. 

By supporting  `OMP_NUM_THREADS` it is easier for operators to control all libaries and applications at once.

issue_title: Segfault when importing alongside tensorflow.contrib or pytorch in python (Linux)
issue_body:
Python segfaults when the following code is run (on Ubuntu 16.04 with gcc 5.4.0):
```
import sentencepiece
import tensorflow.contrib
```
The segfault does not happen when they are imported in the reverse order, mysteriously.

Probable cause: the quay.io manylinux1 docker image used by the make_py_wheel.sh script has gcc 4.8 (and consequently so does sentencepiece Linux wheel on PyPI), which can be incompatible with binaries made using gcc > 4.9.2. Hence pulling sentencepiece into the same address space as binaries made using 4.9.2 may cause issues like segmentation faults.

More discussion in the pytorch repo (same issue encountered): https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/8358

The ideal solution seems to be to compile and release sentencepiece made with gcc 4.9. If you can update make_py_wheel.sh and/or upload a new version to PyPI, that would be awesome!

issue_body:
Hi,

With python 3, when invoking the `parse()` function on turtle files as part of our testing suite, we get the following warning:
```
ResourceWarning: unclosed file <_io.BufferedReader name='<PATH_TO_TTL_FILE>'>
``` 
The full test report is available [here](https://travis-ci.org/incf-nidash/nidmresults-fsl/builds/157843978).

  g2.parse(local_ttl_file, format='turtle')
.
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Ran 2 tests in 3.709s

OK
```

Where `test_rdflib_warning.py ` contains:
```
from rdflib.graph import Graph
import urllib.request
import unittest


class TestResourceWarning(unittest.TestCase):

    def test_url(self):
        # No warning
        ttl_url = \
            "https://provenance.ecs.soton.ac.uk/store/documents/114154.ttl"

        g1 = Graph()
        g1.parse(ttl_url, format='turtle')

            "https://provenance.ecs.soton.ac.uk/store/documents/114154.ttl"

if __name__ == '__main__':

```

Is it possible that the turtle parser is not closing the turtle file after reading it?

I'm an artist, and my web site allows viewing my work as thumbnails or in a "news feed" view (similar to Instagram or Facebook). I'm using `fa-th` for thumbnails, but there's no good icon for the "feed" view. `fa-bars`, `fa-photo`, and `th-list` aren't a good match. The one I ended up using is `fa-square`, but it's pretty generic.

So I'd like to propose a new icon, `fa-card`, that somewhat resembles a Polaroid photograph -- a square-ish rectangle outline, with a filled rectangle inside, vertically aligned close to the top.

![example-fa-card](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/2041718/11916252/15d189e0-a694-11e5-9317-30c695105a0a.png)

Both Semantic UI and Google's Material Design refer to this UI convention as a "card," and that seems like a good name since `fa-feed` is already taken for RSS/Atom feeds.

https://www.google.com/design/spec/components/cards.html

Xcode and iOS require the font's full name to match font-family names.

I spent a couple hours this morning trying to get Font Awesome 5 Pro Light & Light working in Cordova for iOS and ran into an issue where the full name didn't match the css font-family: preventing it from being used in xcode.

issue_title: Add stacked plus and minus icons
issue_body:
Add miniature plus and minus icons that reside to the lower right to Font Awesome.

The issue: there exists user-plus and cart-plus and though the plus icons are in different positions, most things can get by with a smaller plus icon to the lower-right. There are many icons without a plus associated with it. There are no minus icons for them (we could stack on the ban icon, but it may mean a different thing depending on context).

Now, It is possible to do this with css tricks, but I think scaling and rotation would be much easier and cleaner if these icons were developed in the same size as everything else that's stackable, but made smaller and pushed to the lower-right.

It has the benefit of being styled and colored so we can do green pluses and red minuses, etc. I think many web apps will definitely benefit from this kind of modularity.

It also has the benefit that if you keep them to one quadrant you can rotate them to get the other 4 positions. This saves you from creating a new quadrant for each plus and minus.

They could be called fa-mini-plus and fa-mini-minus.

I was wondering if you would consider the following name changes in your icon set :

```
* icon-collapse ----> icon-expand-down
* icon-collapse-top ----> icon-expand-up
* icon-expand ----> icon-expand-right
* NEW ICON ----> icon-expand-left

* icon-expand-alt ----> icon-expand-plus
* icon-collapse-alt ----> icon-expand-minus
```

These naming conventions are more consistent.

Also, they have the additional advantage of being able to use an alias 'icon-collapse' as a reference to two such icons and use a 'collapsed' modified to pick select which of both icons to use. In fact, you would be able to use the 'icon-collapse' with the 'collapsed' modifier on any two icons in the icon set.

I've been using the 'icon-caret-down' and 'icon-caret-right' for collapsibles in Cascade Framework ( http://www.cascade-framework.com/ ) using the technique described above. The suggested name change would also allow me to continue using Font Awesome without any changes to the naming conventions of either project.

issue_title: Question about percentiles in Bayesian early stopping
issue_body:
As I understood from code, percentiles values for regular delta and group sequential delta are using t statistics.
 e.g. in the result object, "pctile" could be (2.5, 50, 97.5), "value" can be the corresponding t statistics (0.1, 0.8, 0.1).
 
On the other hand, percentiles values for two Bayesian delta are using a fixed 0.95 credible interval.
In the result object, "pctile" is always ("lower", "upper"), "value" is the corresponding index of posterior distribution.

I think they are two completely different concept, and if I understood correctly, should we put them into different columns in the result object?

To represent duration in the `time_since_treatment` column, we have 2 options:
- put numerical values into `time_since_treatment` and persist the time unit in the metadata [non-intuitive and probably error-prone]
- represent duration directly with the pandas dtype Timedelta [preferred]

issue_title: System incorrectly rebooted when patroni service stopped with watchdog enabled.
issue_body:
A regression has been introduced by commit 5da0e12353a2e2462437ff36c6512d6ae8ea7142

This is because it is possible for an exception to be raised at https://github.com/zalando/patroni/commit/5da0e12353a2e2462437ff36c6512d6ae8ea7142#diff-74fbda32f108239eb16ce7417ef10085R85

The  exception is (`psutil.NoSuchProcess`)(`psutil.NoSuchProcess process no longer exists`).

This means the watchdog is not correctly closed. Which then causes the system to get reset.

It can be replicated easily by enabling the watchdog then stop the patroni service.

issue_title: Calcul de la PPA - problème avec les dates d'entrée en vigueur
issue_body:
La PPA est calculée en fonction des revenus du trimestre précédent.  Le montant touché au mois N est en fait la moyenne de ce qui aurait pu être perçu par l'agent durant les 3 derniers mois en fonction de ses conditions d'éligibilité pendant cette période.
Pour cela, les dates d'entrée en vigueur des paramètres openfisca ont été antidaté de 3 mois afin de calculer la variable "ppa_fictive" et ainsi, permettre le calcul de la PPA dès le 1er janvier 2016.
Dans les barèmes IPP, nous n'avons pas cette possibilité d'action sur les dates, ce qui bloque le processus de fusion des paramètres IPP/openfisca. 
Les deux solutions pour l'instant envisagées sont : 
- séparer le calcul par des _if_ en faisant appel aux paramètres du "RSA activité" avant le début du 2e trimestre de 2016 et ensuite à ceux de la PPA.
- séparer le calcul par des _if_ en jouant sur la période à l'aide des "simulation.legislation_at(period.start)" en avançant de 3mois la date de début.
@benjello

issue_title: Annotations with special characters in name are silently ignored
issue_body:
Consider this code:

    s = xray_recorder.begin_subsegment('test')
    s.put_annotation('log.mydata', 42)

It is executed without any error or exception, but the annotation just does not appear in XRay console.
If I look at the raw segment data I will see `"annotations": {}` - empty object for annotations. Looks like it is either an issue with XRay backend (silently ignores values with name containing special characters like dot `.` or colon `:`) or an issue with docs & this framework (docs should mention this limitation, and the framework should prohibit such names).

(Edited for clarity)
We were taking a look at 1D convolutions now, and it looks like they aren't doing what we would expect according to Kim 2014: http://aclweb.org/anthology/D/D14/D14-1181.pdf

For example, for conv1d, we would expect:
* Input X is a {L,M} matrix.
* There are N filters F, each of size {L,O}.
* The computation result Y is an {N,M-O+1} matrix. Y_{i,j} is equal to the dot product of filter F[i] and matrix slice X[:,j:j+O].

On the other hand, what we have now is:
* Input X is a {L,M} matrix.
* There are L filters F, each of size {1,O}.
* The computation result Y is an {L, M-O+1} matrix, where Y_{i,j} is equal to the dot product of filter F[i] and matrix slice F[i,j:j+O].

In other words, filters are run row-wise over the input instead of being calculated with respect to the whole input.

issue_title: Inconsistent usage of pointers and references
issue_body:
As it stands, most of the builder classes in `dynet/` use `Model*` in their constructors, whereas most of the builder-like classes in `examples/` use `Model&`. On the other hand, `ComputationGraph`s are largely used as references throughout the code base, whereas there are a small number of pointers (some of which are unused, as in `dynet/treelstm.h`). There's even a mismatch in the current version of `c2w.h`, where (among other issues) a `ComputationGraph*` is being used where a `ComputationGraph&` is expected by `RNNBuilder`.

I'm not sure I can speak for the internal DyNet code, but for all of the builders and example models, is there any compelling reason why we shouldn't be using references to `Model`s and `ComputationGraph`s throughout? If not, I'd be happy to make a pull request to this effect for consistency.

(Possibly related to issue #90.)

issue_title: TODO: Change Unit() to support compound units

It is often the case that a user may want to work in terms of concentration say µg/mL. Currently `Unit()` does not support dimensional division nor multiplication.

Some ideas have been batted around to integrate Pint https://pint.readthedocs.org/en/0.6/ into the Autoprotocol-python library to build out the `Unit()` class with this support. It would be ideal to not introduce breaking changes.

It would be great to have some discussion about this here.

issue_title: Django freezes when using Stackdriver logging
issue_labels: [{'id': 311535631, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20logging', 'name': 'api: logging', 'color': 'c0c0c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604244, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p1', 'name': 'priority: p1', 'color': 'ffa03e', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604235, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/status:%20acknowledged', 'name': 'status: acknowledged', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
OS: `MacOS 10.11.6`
Python: `Python 2.7.10`
Package: 
```
Name: google-cloud-logging
Version: 0.23.1
Summary: Python Client for Stackdriver Logging
Home-page: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python
Author: Google Cloud Platform
Author-email: jjg+google-cloud-python@google.com
License: Apache 2.0
Location: /Users/briandeheus/.virtualenvs/proj/lib/python2.7/site-packages
Requires: gapic-google-cloud-logging-v2, google-cloud-core, grpcio
```

## Steps to reproduce

1. start a new Django project
2. set up your logging as follows:
```
LOGGING = {
    'version': 1,
    'handlers': {
        'stackdriver': {
            'class': 'google.cloud.logging.handlers.CloudLoggingHandler',
            'client': logging.Client()
        }
    },
    'loggers': {
        '': {
            'handlers': ['stackdriver'],
            'level': 'INFO',
            'propagate': True,
        }
}
```

Your app will freeze after a few seconds. I've narrowed it down by adding some print statements to the stackdriver module:

```
_start: Starting
_start: Started
_Run: RUN WORKER
_Run: NOT STOPPING
_Run: WAIT
[2017-03-02 08:18:44,595] DEBUG Skipping environment variable credential check because profile name was explicitly set.
Enqueue: Acquiring
Enqueue: Acquired
Enqueue: Notifying
Enqueue: Notified
Enqueue: Releasing
Enqueue: Released
[2017-03-02 08:18:44,595] DEBUG Looking for credentials via: env
_Run: COMMITING
Commit: BATCH COMMIT
 Enqueue: Acquiring
Commit: WRITE ENTRIES
```

The Worker class has some modified methods to achieve this:

```python
    def _run(self):
        """The entry point for the worker thread.

        Loops until ``stopping`` is set to :data:`True`, and commits batch
        entries written during :meth:`enqueue`.
        """
        try:
            self._entries_condition.acquire()
            self.started = True
            print "_Run: RUN WORKER"
            while not self.stopping:
                print "_Run: NOT STOPPING"
                if len(self.batch.entries) == 0:
                    print "_Run: WAIT"
                    # branch coverage of this code extremely flaky
                    self._entries_condition.wait()  # pragma: NO COVER

                if len(self.batch.entries) > 0:
                    print "_Run: COMMITING"
                    self.batch.commit()

            print "_Run: STOPPING"
        finally:
            print "_Run: RELEASING"
            self._entries_condition.release()

        # main thread may be waiting for worker thread to finish writing its
        # final entries. here we signal that it's done.
        self._stop_condition.acquire()
        self._stop_condition.notify()
        self._stop_condition.release()

    def _start(self):
        """Called by this class's constructor

        This method is responsible for starting the thread and registering
        the exit handlers.
        """
        try:
            print "_start: Starting"
            self._entries_condition.acquire()
            self._thread = threading.Thread(
                target=self._run, name=self.name)
            self._thread.setDaemon(False)
            self._thread.start()
            print "_start: Started"
        finally:
            self._entries_condition.release()
            atexit.register(self._stop)

    def enqueue(self, record, message):
        """Queues up a log entry to be written by the background thread."""
        try:
            print "Enqueue: Acquiring"
            self._entries_condition.acquire()
            if self.stopping:
                print "Enqueue: Stopping"
                return
            print "Enqueue: Acquired"
            info = {'message': message, 'python_logger': record.name}
            self.batch.log_struct(info, severity=record.levelname)
            print "Enqueue: Notifying"
            self._entries_condition.notify()
            print "Enqueue: Notified"
        finally:
            print "Enqueue: Releasing"
            self._entries_condition.release()
            print "Enqueue: Released"
```

I've tried both the `SyncTransport` and the `BackgroundThreadTransport` but both lock up. Whether running your project in the Django development mode or using uWSGI, the end result is always the same.

这里我们采用HaProxy来做转发工具
首先安装HaProxy
Centos使用
```yum install haproxy```
Debian使用
```vi  /etc/apt/sources.list```
添加如下内容
```deb http://ftp.us.debian.org/debian/ wheezy-backports main```
然后
```apt-get update
apt-get install haproxy```
接下来设置配置文件
```vi /etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg```
清空配置文件后，输入如下内容
```global
ulimit-n  51200
defaults
log global
mode    tcp
option  dontlognull
timeout connect 1000ms
timeout client 150000ms
timeout server 150000ms
listen status
bind 0.0.0.0:1080
mode http
log global
stats refresh 30s
stats uri /admin?stats
stats realm Private lands
stats auth admin:password
stats hide-version
frontend ssin
bind *:2000-3305
default_backend ssout
backend ssout
server server1 SS服务器的IP maxconn 204800```

issue_title: SNS Subscription appears to be incomplete
issue_body:
I'm working on a project using AWS::SNS::Subscription

Here's the CFN documentation for reference:
http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-resource-sns-subscription.html#cfn-sns-topicarn

Reviewing the troposphere/sns.py it looks like Subscription is incomplete:
```
class Subscription(AWSProperty):
    props = {
        'Endpoint': (basestring, True),
        'Protocol': (basestring, True),
    }
```
I believe this should be:
```
class Subscription(AWSObject):
    resource_type = "AWS::SNS::Subscription"

    props = {
        'Endpoint': (basestring, True),
        'Protocol': (basestring, True),
        'TopicArn': (basestring, True),

```

issue_title: Python 2: SyntaxError when installing distributions with coding hints in setup.py
issue_body:
I think this is a regression introduced in #231, which was released in pex 1.1.7.  I cannot reproduce this problem on pex 1.1.6.  Affects Python 2 only.

Reproduce with:

```
wget -q 'https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/releases/download/v1.1.7/pex27'
chmod a+x pex27
./pex27 backoff
```

Stack trace:

```
**** Failed to install backoff-1.1.0. stdout:

**** Failed to install backoff-1.1.0. stderr:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 7, in <module>
  File "setup.py", line 0
SyntaxError: encoding declaration in Unicode string

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/private/tmp/pex/pex27/.bootstrap/_pex/pex.py", line 328, in execute
  File "/private/tmp/pex/pex27/.bootstrap/_pex/pex.py", line 260, in _wrap_coverage
  File "/private/tmp/pex/pex27/.bootstrap/_pex/pex.py", line 292, in _wrap_profiling
  File "/private/tmp/pex/pex27/.bootstrap/_pex/pex.py", line 371, in _execute
  File "/private/tmp/pex/pex27/.bootstrap/_pex/pex.py", line 429, in execute_entry
  File "/private/tmp/pex/pex27/.bootstrap/_pex/pex.py", line 447, in execute_pkg_resources
  File "/Users/saj/.pex/install/pex-1.1.7-py2.py3-none-any.whl.1089a1bbad10037ad29421de8a162a77c90c8133/pex-1.1.7-py2.py3-none-any.whl/pex/bin/pex.py", line 538, in main
    pex_builder = build_pex(reqs, options, resolver_options_builder)
  File "/Users/saj/.pex/install/pex-1.1.7-py2.py3-none-any.whl.1089a1bbad10037ad29421de8a162a77c90c8133/pex-1.1.7-py2.py3-none-any.whl/pex/bin/pex.py", line 489, in build_pex
    resolveds = resolver.resolve(resolvables)
  File "/Users/saj/.pex/install/pex-1.1.7-py2.py3-none-any.whl.1089a1bbad10037ad29421de8a162a77c90c8133/pex-1.1.7-py2.py3-none-any.whl/pex/resolver.py", line 200, in resolve
    dist = self.build(package, resolvable.options)
  File "/Users/saj/.pex/install/pex-1.1.7-py2.py3-none-any.whl.1089a1bbad10037ad29421de8a162a77c90c8133/pex-1.1.7-py2.py3-none-any.whl/pex/resolver.py", line 257, in build
    dist = super(CachingResolver, self).build(package, options)
  File "/Users/saj/.pex/install/pex-1.1.7-py2.py3-none-any.whl.1089a1bbad10037ad29421de8a162a77c90c8133/pex-1.1.7-py2.py3-none-any.whl/pex/resolver.py", line 168, in build
    raise Untranslateable('Package %s is not translateable by %s' % (package, translator))
pex.resolver.Untranslateable: Package SourcePackage(u'file:///Users/saj/.pex/build/backoff-1.1.0.tar.gz') is not translateable by ChainedTranslator(WheelTranslator, EggTranslator, SourceTranslator)
```

Even if we were to ignore the problem above, I don't think it is right to assume a UTF-8 encoding on all files.

From [PEP 263](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0263/):
> If a Unicode string with a coding declaration is passed to compile(), a SyntaxError will be raised.

From the same PEP:
> The builtin compile() API will be enhanced to accept Unicode as input. 8-bit string input is subject to the standard procedure for encoding detection as described above.

Instead of opening `setup.py` as a text file and decoding it ourselves, it sounds like it should be possible to hand a binary stream directly to the `compile()` builtin and let Python deal with this muck.
Docs here: https://github.com/z4r/python-coveralls

issue_title: Add an option to prepend `.gitignore` to --pants-ignore
issue_body:
The `--pants-ignore` option causes pants to ignore files and directories: it uses the same syntax as `.gitignore` because it is implemented using the [ignore crate](https://crates.io/crates/ignore). The ignore crate also supports consuming `.gitignore` files from disk, but we're currently not using that support.

This ticket would deal with adding a pants level option next to `--pants-ignore` that would enable using the `.gitignore` file in addition to the explicit patterns in `--pants-ignore`. If the `.gitignore` file patterns were applied first, this would allow for maximum generality: with that option enabled, `--pants-ignore` would be a superset of `.gitignore`, but because negation syntax is supported, you could negate patterns in `--pants-ignore` if you wanted them ignored by git, but not by pants.

`--pants-ignore` is declared here: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/2d4e61753d3896ee969ba239cb0a66c36d781f84/src/python/pants/option/global_options.py#L127-L131 and ends up being used here: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/2d4e61753d3896ee969ba239cb0a66c36d781f84/src/rust/engine/fs/src/lib.rs#L388-L393

      self.send_response(200)
      self.end_headers()

def setup_delayed_server(delay):

timeout = setup_delayed_server(1)

```

Right now, the [checkstyle tool is shaded](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/checkstyle.py#L42-L56), although the `com.puppycrawl.tools.checkstyle` package is excluded from shading to keep module names as-documented by checkstyle.  This arrangment does not help custom extension added to the classpath though.  Those wil be shaded and users then must adapt their coding_style.xml like so:

Before
```xml
<module name="Checker">
    <module name="com.twitter.common.checkstyle.SplitSuppressionFilter">
      <property name="files" value="${checkstyle.suppression.files}"/>
    </module>

    <module name="SuppressionCommentFilter">
      <property name="offCommentFormat" value="CHECKSTYLE\:OFF ([\w\|]+)"/>
      <property name="onCommentFormat" value="CHECKSTYLE\:ON ([\w\|]+)"/>
      <property name="checkFormat" value="$1"/>
    </module>
...
```

After
```xml
<module name="Checker">
    <module name="__shaded_by_pants__.com.twitter.common.checkstyle.SplitSuppressionFilter">
      <property name="files" value="${checkstyle.suppression.files}"/>
    </module>

    <module name="SuppressionCommentFilter">
      <property name="offCommentFormat" value="CHECKSTYLE\:OFF ([\w\|]+)"/>
      <property name="onCommentFormat" value="CHECKSTYLE\:ON ([\w\|]+)"/>
      <property name="checkFormat" value="$1"/>
    </module>
...
```

Someone configuring pants should not need to know or figure out the magic `__shaded_by_pants__` package prefix added by the pants tool shading.  The should be able to opt their checker package(s) out of shading instead in some way.

STRUCTURED: time=2016-11-03T14:22:49.706615-00 pid=28474 ``` I am not exactly sure why the resources are missing and what it means. But having an infinite retry for the resource that's not there doesn't make sense. I have a patch to fix this, but I am not sure why exactly are we missing the resources. Is it of importance, missing these resources. Also, I want to add that earlier wal-e were using some other container for the wal-e backups. We recently changed it without moving old files to the new container. Does that affect the wal-e somehow with respect to this and is it trying to delete resources from the old container? There should be a way to reset state with respect to this. Full log: [error_log.txt](https://github.com/wal-e/wal-e/files/569252/error_log.txt)

issue_title: Determine the overall placement of tables
issue_body:
Hi
I'm working on a python script which should be able to delete specific tables and paragraphs from a docx document. But I have a problem in that I cannot find out how to determine where in a document (relative to the paragraphs) a table is placed. Therefore I will have trouble finding and deleting the surrounding paragraphs after I have located a table that needs to be removed. 
Is there any way to detect the flow of the document?

I have looked at the paragraph._p structure because  issue #156 shows that this could be used to move a table in between specific paragraphs using the addnext() method. However, the getnext function of all my paragraphs seem to point to other paragraphs.

Currently, Column.width and Cell.width need to be set separately to get table column widths to appear properly in most WordprocessingML applications (e.g. Word, LibreOffice).

Add logic to `Column` such that assigning a width to `Column.width` causes the width of each cell in that column to be updated to match.

Note that merged cells will need to be set to the sum of the widths of the columns they span. A naive approach of just setting each cell to the width of its column will not produce the desired behavior.

I have some difficulties setting a style for tables.

I look into the `styles.xml` to find exactly what string I have to copy to my code:

```xml
...
<w:name w:val="Grid Table 7 Colorful Accent 1"/>
...
```

However, it's impossible to set it:
```python
>>> from docx import Document
>>> doc = Document()
>>> table = doc.add_table(1, 1)
>>> table.style = "Grid Table 7 Colorful Accent 1"
Traceback (most recent call last):

What is wrong?

Could you please inset licence into distributed source code?

Because BSD licence requires including its text with all derivative works. So without including a proper full license text it is making difficult or impossible for anyone to comply with desired license terms.

There was discussion on old mailing list - http://thread.gmane.org/gmane.comp.search.snowball/1463

Thanks,
Marek

phantomjs-prebuilt

When i try again,

issue_title: Not able to see protractor question exercises in tablet
issue_body:
### Observed behavior
In the KA resource, the protractor isn't visible on the tablets, although we can see the up and down arrows which is used to move the protractor upside down. This issue is only occurring on tablets.

- Channel ID: 8fc515793aad4e52b490b03c48ad21de -Maharahstra State English Medium
- Path of the topic: BalBharati---> Class 5 --->Angles---> KA Resources
- Exercise name: Measure angles


### Expected behavior
It must show whole protractor image in tablet.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Login with learner.
2. Follow the path BalBharati---> Class 5 --->Angles---> KA Resource  of channel ID 8fc515793aad4e52b490b03c48ad21de(Maharahstra State English Medium)

issue_title: "Oh, cool, I've already earned 500 points, and haven't even started watching!"
issue_body:
When I saw the greyed out points box, my initial (and post-initial reaction) was that I had already earned those points, and since nothing changed when I started watching, that assumption was reinforced.

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/618416/25973804/88b2b66c-365a-11e7-88e9-645ae8535a9a.png)

Is it necessary to show "potential points" before they've been earned? If so, is there some way we can indicate more clearly that they're unearned? E.g. "0 / 500" or something?

## Summary

Console `[Vue warn]` while playing MP3 file, playback has no issues.

## System information

 - Version: latest develop
 - Operating system: Ubuntu 16.10
 - Browser: Firefox & Chrome (somewhat different warn, though, see screenshots)
 - Content channel ID: 8b4d3e6d3d4842ba8ea658335b5dd252


## Traceback or relevant snippet from server.log or browser console

```
[Vue warn]: Error when evaluating setter "rawTime": TypeError: this.$els.audio is undefined

[Vue warn]: Error when evaluating setter "rawTime": TypeError: Cannot set property 'currentTime' of undefined
```

## How to reproduce

1. Import channel
1. Go to `Explore - Science - Audio`
1. Load any of the MP3 files.

issue_title: Having opinel use the standard `mfa_serial` AWS configuration variable.
issue_body:
Currently opinel looks for the custom configuration variable `aws_mfa_serial` in the ~/.aws/credentials file to get the arn of the MFA device.

For instance, my current `~/.aws/credentials` file might look like this:

```
[development]
aws_access_key_id=foo
aws_secret_access_key=bar
aws_mfa_serial=arn:aws:iam::XXXXXXXXXXXX:mfa/john.doe
```

However, the AWS/boto3 configuration variables have changed a bit since opinel was first introduced. When assuming roles, boto3 [now looks for the arn of the MFA device via the `mfa_serial` variable in the `~/.aws/config file](http://boto3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/guide/configuration.html#assume-role-provider).

It would be nice if opinel used the `mfa_serial` variable in the ~/.aws/config file, too, both for assuming roles and other standard operations.

So a typical configuration might look something like this…

In `~/.aws/credentials`:

```
[development]
aws_access_key_id=foo
aws_secret_access_key=bar
```

In `~/.aws/config`:

```
[profile development]
mfa_serial=arn:aws:iam::XXXXXXXXXXXX:mfa/john.doe
region=us-east-1
output=json


[profile crossaccount]
source_profile=development
role_arn=arn:aws:iam::XXXXXXXXXXXX:role/whatever
mfa_serial=arn:aws:iam::XXXXXXXXXXXX:mfa/john.doe
region=us-east-1
output=json
```

For semver to make any sense, we really need to actually define what our public API is. We probably want to do this both through documentation and through the de-facto Python standard of underscore-prefixing (though that will likely break stuff again, le sight, but if people are using the private API they'll likely complain when we do break it in some 1.x release).

Currently almost all our error messages are passed through gettext. Do we actually want localizable error messages? To me, at least, this seems an anti-feature.

cc/ @ambv and @jgraham

issue_title: Article fields in the wrong language are getting indexed
issue_body:
At [search_indexes.py](https://github.com/aldryn/aldryn-newsblog/blob/master/aldryn_newsblog/search_indexes.py) there are `safe_translation_getter()` calls without current language argument (and it looks like _parler_ does not try to detect the current language inside that method either). So, article's filelds translated in first available language are sometimes indexed in the _Solr_ core for another language.
The _aldryn-search_ was built with _hvad_ in mind, so we probably need to take some extra steps in index methods to properly index translatable models.
@yakky, can you try to reproduce this and adjust the index?
CC: @czpython @intellectronica

issue_title: Supporting creating applications with a json file (or json-formatted string, or json object)
issue_body:
The `marathon.MarathonApp` object can be built by passing all the parameters defining an application.

It would be nice if there was a way to create `marathon.MarathonApp` with json content directly.

A workaround, given a json object, would be to use `marathon.MarathonApp(**myapp_json)`, but there would still be issues for method parameters using snake-case and json keys using camelCase:

E.g.,
```
TypeError: __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'upgradeStrategy'
```

issue_title: Feature request: cosine annealing scheduler with warm restarts
issue_body:
Currently, the pytorch implementation of [the cosine annealing learning rate schedule](https://pytorch.org/docs/stable/optim.html#torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR) does not actually include warm restarts, which was the key idea in the paper referenced from the docs:

> It has been proposed in [SGDR: Stochastic Gradient Descent with Warm Restarts](https://arxiv.org/abs/1608.03983). Note that this only implements the cosine annealing part of SGDR, and not the restarts.

There is currently a [pull request open on pytorch](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/6130) that addresses this issue, but it is unclear if or when it will be merged and released. Since the logic is relatively simple, and there are lots of potential variants of the algorithm, I propose implementing a custom cosine annealing schedule with warm restarts within AllenNLP.

I've actually already implemented this for my own research, so I'd be willing to submit a PR. It looks something like this:

```python
@LearningRateScheduler.register("cosine_with_restarts")
class CosineWithRestarts(_LRScheduler):
    """
    Cosine annealing with restarts.

    Parameters
    ----------
    optimizer : torch.optim.Optimizer

    T_max : int
        The maximum number of iterations within the first cycle.

    eta_min : float, optional (default: 0)
        The minimum learning rate.

    last_epoch : int, optional (default: -1)
        The index of the last epoch.

    """

    def __init__(self,
                 optimizer: torch.optim.Optimizer,
                 T_max: int,
                 eta_min: float = 0.,
                 last_epoch: int = -1,
                 factor: float = 1.) -> None:
        # pylint: disable=invalid-name
        self.T_max = T_max
        self.eta_min = eta_min
        self.factor = factor
        self._last_restart: int = 0
        self._cycle_counter: int = 0
        self._cycle_factor: float = 1.
        self._updated_cycle_len: int = T_max
        self._initialized: bool = False
        super(CosineWithRestarts, self).__init__(optimizer, last_epoch)

    def get_lr(self):
        """Get updated learning rate."""
        # HACK: We need to check if this is the first time get_lr() was called, since
        # we want to start with step = 0, but _LRScheduler calls get_lr with
        # last_epoch + 1 when initialized.
        if not self._initialized:
            self._initialized = True
            return self.base_lrs

        step = self.last_epoch + 1
        self._cycle_counter = step - self._last_restart

        lrs = [
            (
                self.eta_min + ((lr - self.eta_min) / 2) *
                (
                    np.cos(
                        np.pi *
                        ((self._cycle_counter) % self._updated_cycle_len) /
                        self._updated_cycle_len
                    ) + 1
                )
            ) for lr in self.base_lrs
        ]

        if self._cycle_counter % self._updated_cycle_len == 0:
            # Adjust the cycle length.
            self._cycle_factor *= self.factor
            self._cycle_counter = 0
            self._updated_cycle_len = int(self._cycle_factor * self.T_max)
            self._last_restart = step

        return lrs
```

issue_title: Hadoop collector, open read-only if not truncating
issue_body: Currently the hadoop collector always opens hadoop metrics files with `'r+'` ... if the settings have `truncate = False` the file should be open `'r'` only to avoid permissions issues under certain circumstances. There is no reason to make the files writeable to the diamond user if not necessary.

{"data": [1,2,3,4], "zero_data": []}

lib/python2.7/site-packages/jmespath/functions.pyc in _func_avg(self, arg)
    177     @builtin_function({'types': ['array-number']})
    178     def _func_avg(self, arg):
--> 179         return sum(arg) / float(len(arg))
    180
    181     @builtin_function({'types': [], 'variadic': True})

ZeroDivisionError: float division by zero
```

issue_title: All of the Canadian clients return datetime data type for 'timestamp'
issue_body:
Each of the ISO / balancing authority clients returns a `list` of `dict` objects with key / values described at: https://pyiso.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage.html#clients However, there's no explicit mention of what data type the value for the `timestamp` key should be. In all of the Canadian clients, a timezone-aware UTC `datetime` value is returned. After discussion with WattTimer folks, returning `pandas.Timestamp` in UTC is the correct data type (for the time being). 

Update all returned values for 'timestamp' dictionary entries to `pandas.Timestamp` in the Canadian clients.

issue_title: docs: SSH keys when using vagrant
issue_body:
Working through the vagrant install, I ran into an issue understanding which SSH keys needed to be added.

The Vagrant instructions ask you to generate an SSH key, but then have you using the vagrant insecure key. When you move into "Installing the Deis Platform" it has you set the private key for the servers to talk to one another and references "~/.ssh/deis" which is the key you create in the Vagrant instructions.

This is confusing since you should be using the vagrant insecure key at this point.

I'd suggest modifying the Vagrant install instructions to not have the user generate a deis key and explain that they will be using the Vagrant insecure key locally.

issue_title: Proxying web request causes timeout when target is another Deis application
issue_body:
One of our projects is attempting to use a fairly well known node.js HTTP proxy module:

https://github.com/nodejitsu/node-http-proxy

to redirect incoming requests to a particular microservice in the backend to consolidate the API surface to one domain and provide application auth services in a central location. The source of what this app is trying to do is here:

https://github.com/nitrogenjs/frontdoor/blob/master/server.js

and the relevant code that this http-proxy module is using to proxy is here:

https://github.com/nodejitsu/node-http-proxy/blob/master/lib/http-proxy/passes/web-incoming.js

(see "stream" function)

This request aims for parity with [OOM Killer](http://linux-mm.org/OOM_Killer) behaviour.

The fact that Heroku does a straight SIGKILL on [r15](https://devcenter.heroku.com/articles/error-codes#r15-memory-quota-vastly-exceeded) ram exhaustion has always baffled me, seeing as [r14](https://devcenter.heroku.com/articles/error-codes#r14-memory-quota-exceeded) is logged but never signalled to the offending container. If this was fixed and I'm woefully clueless please do let me know.

I propose the same signalling from #3086 be applied to ram exhaustion. For now, I don't think we need to set hard and soft limit triggers (unless these are already in place), but simply kill with timeout. I'm assuming that if coreos itself runs out of ram OOM Killer will do the expected rapid-fire SIGTERM && SIGKILL.

I don't see this as an excuse to avoid crash-only design patterns, but knowing why a crash has happened at the app level sure does help debug it. Signals are my friends.

issue_body:
`globus endpoint role list <uuid>` and `globus endpoint permission list <uuid>` assume that every entry in the list will resolve to an identity so the `username` can be printed. However, Transfer allows ACLs and roles to be assigned to arbitrary UUIDs.

issue_title: TransferData and DeleteData should not call transfer_client.get_submission_id() in their __init__()s if kwargs already has a submission_id
issue_body:
If the caller provides a `submission_id`, this is a wasted call, adds unnecessary delay, and prevents dry-run testings without a valid token.

Additionally, if the caller explicitly passes a `submission_id` kwarg, but its value is `None`, then the behavior should be the same as if they passed no `submission_id` at all (i.e. a `get_submission_id()` call should indeed be made and the `self.update(kwargs)` call that happens at the end of initialization should not clobber it).

We should probably just explicitly name `submission_id` in the `__init__()` arguments list with a default of `None`, and explicitly do the right thing in the function body before the `self.update()` call happens (similar to `sync_level` and `label`).

issue_title: Support for returning data within a time range
issue_body:
Would be great to introduce the ability to return data within a time range. 

This functionality isn't available in the New Relic API Explorer, but works if you're calling it manually via CURL as per this doc https://docs.newrelic.com/docs/apis/rest-api-v2/requirements/extracting-metric-data

For example, the below would return the metrics between 05/09/2014 - 11/09/2015 in 1 hour periods;
```
-d'names[]=from=2015-09-05T10:00:00+00:00&to=2015-09-11T10:00:00+00:00&period=3600'
```

Python isn't my primary language, but I'lltry and submit a pull request myself unless someone thinks they can do it before me.

issue_title: Re-creating conda env with custom channels results in HTTP error (perhaps needlessly?)
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...** - [X] bug report - [X] feature request ## Current Behavior I created an environment on one machine and wanted to replicate that environment. On the original machine, I exported the environment details using: `conda env export > my-env.yml`. On the new machine, running `conda env create -n my-env-copy --file my-env.yml` resulted in at HTTP error (as was the case in #7535): ```shell CondaHTTPError: HTTP 000 CONNECTION FAILED for url ``` ### Steps to Reproduce Create a new environment from an exported yaml file, which includes an http address under the channels sub-section. ## Expected Behavior The channel should not only be searched for via the given http address in the `env.yml` file, but also perhaps via pure channel name, which I guess can often be parsed from the http address. In my case, the http was: `- https://conda.binstar.org/bashtage` I changed the same line in the yaml file to `bashtage` - which then worked as expected. Could a fix/patch be as simple as something like: ```python channel = ''https://conda.binstar.org/bashtage" # from yaml-reader try: # whatever normally happens: except CondaHTTPError as e: channel_name = channel.split('/')[-1] # overwrite the variable that is passed to the usual method in the try-block above # ... try again, which would either work or raise the same CondaHTTPError # and we don't catch it the second time around! finally: # in case of success using name, and we want to keep users informed... warnings.warn('A message alerting user that channel name was used instead of http address') ``` Alternatively, all this could be performed via the export process, while _creating_ the yaml file. I assume the http address was chosen because it should be static/more robust? ## Environment Information

issue_title: Problem: packages with build features are not updateable by conda update --all
issue_body:
We currently facing the situation, that packages which are built with features like `gcc540` or `vc15` can not updated with a general `conda update --all` but only directly by `conda update package`

To check this behaviour, we create a minimal test package which is built by `conda build --croot builds/ src/`as v1 and later as v2.
Second we create a local conda channel, which was configured in `.condarc` with
```
channels:
  - file:///path/to/conda-test-chan/
```
with `conda install testpkg` version 1 was correctly installed. after this we built v2, put it into our test channel and create a new index. issue_title: Segmentation fault when editing format fields.
issue_body:
### How to reproduce
`sample.vcf.gz` is in the attachment. [sample.vcf.gz](https://github.com/pysam-developers/pysam/files/2081896/sample.vcf.gz)
The error can be reproduced by running the following script in python3:
```
import pysam

in_vcf = '/path/to/sample.vcf.gz'
vcf = pysam.VariantFile(in_vcf, 'r')

for record in vcf:

issue_title: Unable to access S3 files with newer pysam releases (protocol not supported)
issue_body:
Hi,
This may be related to issue #557. I was having trouble getting current versions of pysam to access files on S3, so I took a clean public AMI (ami-86795cfc) running CentOS 7, installed common prerequisites like libcurl-devel, installed Python 3.6.4, and then installed pysam using pip3.6. As had been the case on my usual system, I'm unable to access BAM files stored on S3 using pysam.AlignmentFile, and I get the message "Protocol not supported." I do think this is specifically a pysam issue for two reasons: 1) This problem only exists on newer versions of pysam. When I downgrade to pysam 0.9.1, the problem goes away. 2) When I install the latest version of Samtools, there is no problem interacting with S3 files, suggesting that the problem is not with htslib itself.

issue_title: [Bug] Context menu entries does not work at mouse but at cursor position
issue_body:
Using of a context menu entry should always use the mouse position, not the cursor position.

The easiest implementation may be:

- disable screen updates (if possible)
- save current cursor position
- set cursor position based on mouse cursor
- do the requested action
- restore cursor position
- enable screen updates (if possible)

This applies to nearly all context menu options: (un)intend, calculate offset, duplicate line, ...

## System information


- OpenCobolIDE: 4.6.5
- Python: 3.4.3 (32bits)
- Qt: 5.5.0
- PyQt: 5.5
- pyqode.core: 2.6.8
- pyqode.cobol: 2.6.3
- pyqode.qt: 2.7.dev0
- pygments: 2.0.2
- QDarkStyle: 2.1

cobc (and gcc) in Windows distribtution use /mingw in their internal settings. This leads to conflicts if a local installation is installed on %installdisk%\MinGW.

If possible the included MinGW installation should not use /mingw at all (not sure if this is easy possible) and the installer should check for this specific installation directory (currently %installdisk%\MinGW and warn if it exists.

issue_title: FR for compiler settings: add options for enabling the warnings
issue_body:
I know that OCIDE is in mostly-bugfix mode but I think this one is really very important,

The warning flags are compiler dependent but all versions since at least OpenCOBOL 0.3 support the two groups `-W` (ALL warnings) and `-Wall` most warnings (sounds strange, doesn't it?) ...

It would be really good to have an option group for this in the compiler setting (group caption, * option, (tooltip) :

Warning group:
* none (add single warnings via "Extra compiler flags", if needed)
* -W (enable every warning)
* -Wall (enable most warnings, check compiler documentation for the specific warnings included)

I'd like to see -Wall enabled as new default, too as OCIDE is often used by people not knowing the compiler (or even COBOL) well and stumble over problems which they'd have seen directly with -Wall. But this is up to you.

If you run api('users').getall()

```
'test': {'privilege': '1', 'nopassword': False, 'secret': '', 'role': '', 'format': ''}
```

After digging around, if I change the regex from:

```
    users_re = re.compile(r'username ([^\s]+) privilege (\d+)'
                          r'(?: role ([^\s]+))?'
                          r'(?: (nopassword))?'
                          r'(?: secret ([0,5,7]) (.+))?', re.M)

to

```
    users_re = re.compile(r'username ([^\s]+) privilege (\d+)'
                          r'(?: role ([^\s]+))?'
                          r'(?: (nopassword))?'
                          r'(?: secret (0|5|7|sha512) (.+))?', re.M)

```

issue_title: Curator rollover action does not behave like Elasticsearch rollover API
issue_body:
### Expected Behavior
Curator should have similar controls and behavior as Elasticsearch API

### Actual Behavior
*curator*
When choosing conditions, either one of max_age or max_docs, or both may be used. If both are used, then both conditions must be matched for the rollover to occur.
https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/client/curator/current/rollover.html

*elasticsearch*
Elasticsearch also supports `max_size`, and will rollover if *ANY* condition of the three possible conditions is met.

https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/indices-rollover-index.html#CO150-2

issue_title: Add a feature to pull unit_count for filters from index name
issue_body:
We are currently looking into building a flexible log management architecture that gives the log sender some control over how his log events are treated by processing metadata about that log source.
One of those fields is a time to live in days, so how long that event should be kept, before it is deleted.

Events are then stored in indices called logstash-30-yyyy.mm.dd (for a 30 day ttl) or logstash-60-yyyy.mm.dd (for a 60 day ttl) etc.
In order to enable housekeeping on these we'd need to create an action in curator per existing value for ttl (unless I am missing something), which is not desireable.
Would it be of interest to add functionality to specify a regex or something similar to enable curator to dynamically pull the value for unit_count from the index name? I'd be happy to look into this and come up with a proposal of how configuration could look first and work on a PR later on if people think this makes sense.

This question is more focused on the Allocation and Replicas actions, but is it possible to add an option to allow curator to wait for those actions to complete before proceeding to other actions?

For instance, in Allocation add a "wait_for_completion" flag that will run `self.client.cluster.health(level='indices', wait_for_relocating_shards=0)`. The same for Replicas to run `self.client.cluster.health(level='indices', wait_for_status='green')`

issue_title: The server side paging scenario in storage commands is not completed.
issue_body:
The `az storage blob list`, `az storage container list` and `az storage share list` commands all exposed `--marker` and `--num-results` parameters for server-side paging. However, the scenario is never completed because the `next_marker` property is not returned.

The `next_maker` is an opaque string for the next command to retrieve the next page of results, it is the value served to `--marker` parameter. To expose this value, a change is required for the list commands' results. The current return type of list command is a JSON list.

#### Solution 1:
Change the results type from list to dictionary:
```JSON
{
  "items": [
    {
      "content": null,
      "metadata": null,
      "name": "file_1016.txt",
      "properties": {
        "appendBlobCommittedBlockCount": null,
        "blobType": "BlockBlob",
        "contentLength": 264,
        "contentRange": null,
        "contentSettings": {
          "cacheControl": null,
          "contentDisposition": null,
          "contentEncoding": null,
          "contentLanguage": null,
          "contentMd5": "lMf6lT+DBK3I6k9YdydOgg==",
          "contentType": "application/octet-stream"
        },
        "copy": {
          "completionTime": null,
          "id": null,
          "progress": null,
          "source": null,
          "status": null,
          "statusDescription": null
        },
        "etag": "0x8D4B758188BCA6C",
        "lastModified": "2017-06-19T21:13:56+00:00",
        "lease": {
          "duration": null,
          "state": "available",
          "status": "unlocked"
        },
        "pageBlobSequenceNumber": null,
        "serverEncrypted": false
      },
      "snapshot": null
    }
  ],
  "next_marker": "2!76!MDAwMDEzIWZpbGVfMTAxNy50eHQhMDAwMDI4ITk5OTktMTItMzFUMjM6NTk6NTkuOTk5OTk5OVoh"
}
```
Pro: the most completed and concise return result. friendly to JMESPath query.
Con: it's a breaking change.

#### Solution 2:
Keep the list structure, attach the next marker to each blob info.
```JSON
[
    {
      "content": null,
      "metadata": null,
      "name": "file_1016.txt",
      "properties": {
        "appendBlobCommittedBlockCount": null,
        "blobType": "BlockBlob",
        "contentLength": 264,
        "contentRange": null,
        "contentSettings": {
          "cacheControl": null,
          "contentDisposition": null,
          "contentEncoding": null,
          "contentLanguage": null,
          "contentMd5": "lMf6lT+DBK3I6k9YdydOgg==",
          "contentType": "application/octet-stream"
        },
        "copy": {
          "completionTime": null,
          "id": null,
          "progress": null,
          "source": null,
          "status": null,
          "statusDescription": null
        },
        "etag": "0x8D4B758188BCA6C",
        "lastModified": "2017-06-19T21:13:56+00:00",
        "lease": {
          "duration": null,
          "state": "available",
          "status": "unlocked"
        },
        "pageBlobSequenceNumber": null,
        "serverEncrypted": false
      },
      "snapshot": null,
      "next_marker": "2!76!MDAwMDEzIWZpbGVfMTAxNy50eHQhMDAwMDI4ITk5OTktMTItMzFUMjM6NTk6NTkuOTk5OTk5OVoh"
    }
]
```
Pro: not a breaking change. keep the existing structure.
Con: redundant data in output. making query next marker a bit awkward in JMESPath.

#### Solution 3:
Remove the `--result-nums` and `--marker` parameter from CLI command. Since the `ListGenerator` is already doing server side paging, we can leave it to user for the client side paging.

issue_title: DNS zone import should support @ in RDATA
issue_body:
The following is a legitimate DNS record

www 	3600 	IN	CNAME	@

Currently, this causes a Bad Request error.

This should be imported as a CNAME from www.<$ORIGIN> to <$ORIGIN>.  I.e. where @ is used in RDATA, it should be replaced by $ORIGIN.

Another example might be

w   3600   IN  CNAME   www.@

**Note**: this applies anywhere with a domain name in RDATA. For example, SOA, MX, SRV, PTR, NS, CNAME.  It does not apply to the character '@' when used in a TXT record.

issue_title: az storage container create --public-access uses confusing labels creating security risks
issue_body:
CLI Version: 0.10.6
OS Type: Mac
Installation via:  pip

node-cli bug: [#3403](https://github.com/Azure/azure-xplat-cli/issues/3403)

Environment: AzureCloud

Description:
`az storage container create --public-access` uses confusing labels for each type of permission that can be granted, opening up the container to potential security issues with mistaken permissions granting public, wide open read access to the blob when the end user _thinks_ they are assigning the container a _storage role_.

If an Azure infrastructure is being engineered to use Azure Blob storage for Application and Linux OS logging, mistakenly setting a _Blob_ *ACL* on the container while creating it on the `azure-cli`, could open up the sensitive data inside the logs to internet wide read access.  Renaming the *ACL* options to be something more logically aligned to a permissions naming structure instead of a storage type naming structure, would avoid this potentially easy to make mistake.

Steps to reproduce:

Run `-h` for --public-access cli flag

```
az storage container create --public-access -h
                                              [--public-access {container,off,blob}]
```

Expected behavior:

```
az storage container create --public-access -h
                                              [--public-access {Private,ReadOnly,Public}]
```

The permission ACLs would be appropriately named in a logical fashion.  This would ensure the end user gives the storage container the correct permissions.

_Private = Private - no public access_
_ReadOnly = Read Only Access to everyone on the Internet_
_Public = Read & List Access to everyone on the internet_

Actual behavior:

```
az storage container create --public-access -h
                                              [--public-access {container,off,blob}]
```

The end user could very easily mistakenly apply the `blob` ACL to the container thinking they are wanting to create a `blob` type of container.    The permissions flag is the only flag that mentions what type of container you are creating, easily making the *permissions* flag appear to be the *type* flag, which there isnt one.

_Off = Private - no public access_
_Blob = Read Only Access to everyone on the Internet_
_Container = Read & Write Access to everyone on the internet_

issue_title: az acs kubernetes subcommand is missing
issue_body:
I'm attempting to follow https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nhY9XdzNbbY but the kubernetes subcommand doesn't seem to be available after a `pip install azure-cli`

```
$ az acs kubernetes
usage: az acs [-h] {create,delete,show,update,list} ...
az acs: error: argument subcommand: invalid choice: 'kubernetes' (choose from 'create', 'delete', 'show', 'update', 'list')

```

I am currently using msrest 0.4.11 and found the `Deserializer` will raise this exception: `requests.exceptions.ConnectionError`

requests library read the raw socket to get the response body when calling `response.text` [here](https://github.com/Azure/msrest-for-python/blob/2b7d77837c37ba35f2e49df657d66b13cf459b79/msrest/serialization.py#L1100).

if connection/server is not stable, it will raise **ConnectionError** exception from requests library

is it possible to catch this exception in Deserializer?
Because user of Azure SDK will expect the exception is always `CloudError`.

here is the callstack of `msrest 0.4.11` version:
```
  File "D:\home\Python35\lib\site-packages\msrest\paging.py", line 109, in __next__
    self.advance_page()
  File "D:\home\Python35\lib\site-packages\msrest\paging.py", line 96, in advance_page
    self._derserializer(self, self._response)
  File "D:\home\Python35\lib\site-packages\msrest\serialization.py", line 733, in __call__
    data = self._unpack_content(response_data)
  File "D:\home\Python35\lib\site-packages\msrest\serialization.py", line 806, in _unpack_content
    if not raw_data.text:
  File "D:\home\Python35\lib\site-packages\requests\models.py", line 847, in text
    if not self.content:
  File "D:\home\Python35\lib\site-packages\requests\models.py", line 823, in content
    self._content = bytes().join(self.iter_content(CONTENT_CHUNK_SIZE)) or bytes()
  File "D:\home\Python35\lib\site-packages\requests\models.py", line 752, in generate
    raise ConnectionError(e)
```

thanks!

issue_title: Value Error when calling receive_queue_message method
issue_body: Hello, I have been using the azure SDK for some time now. I have never really bothered to look into the codebase until it started throwing a `ValueError` few days ago. The problem comes when calling `receive_queue_message` method which calls other methods. By looking at the code, the error is caused by this line [_create_message](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/master/azure-servicebus/azure/servicebus/_serialization.py#L101). It seems like we are expecting some string that will be cast into a float, then integer and then back to string. However, I am getting something like `max-age=31536000` which will fail to be cast. Have anyone faced the same issue before? Any recommendation is appreciated. Best, Flavio
It's a TODO in the code, right now you won't be able to use proxies if you want to use the new request_session parameter in service management apis.

issue_body: I grab my storage connection string from the portal and create my service with it, but then when I try to call anything I get this error: ``` >>> bs = azure.storage.blob.BlockBlobService(connection_string='...') >>> bs.list_blobs('download') Traceback (most recent call last): File "C:\Python35\lib\site-packages\azure\storage\storageclient.py", line 117, in _perform_request resp = self._filter(request) File "C:\Python35\lib\site-packages\azure\storage\storageclient.py", line 109, in _perform_request_worker return self._httpclient.perform_request(request) File "C:\Python35\lib\site-packages\azure\storage\_http\httpclient.py", line 222, in perform_request raise HTTPError(status, message, respheaders, respbody) azure.storage._http.HTTPError: The requested URI does not represent any resource on the server. During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred: Traceback (most recent call last): File "", line 1, in File "C:\Python35\lib\site-packages\azure\storage\blob\baseblobservice.py", line 1154, in list_blobs resp = self._list_blobs(*args, **kwargs) File "C:\Python35\lib\site-packages\azure\storage\blob\baseblobservice.py", line 1224, in _list_blobs response = self._perform_request(request) File "C:\Python35\lib\site-packages\azure\storage\storageclient.py", line 125, in _perform_request _storage_error_handler(ex) File "C:\Python35\lib\site-packages\azure\storage\_serialization.py", line 130, in _storage_error_handler return _general_error_handler(http_error) File "C:\Python35\lib\site-packages\azure\storage\_error.py", line 71, in _general_error_handler raise AzureHttpError(message, http_error.status) azure.common.AzureHttpError: The requested URI does not represent any resource on the server. InvalidUriThe requested URI does not represent any resource on the server. RequestId:0e8b9823-0001-008d-79e2-7e4356000000 Time:2016-03-15T17:43:04.5101788Zhttps://myaccount.blob.core.windows.net//download?restype=container&comp=list ``` Looking at the UriPath in the result, there seems to be an extra / before the container name, and indeed, if I remove the trailing slash from the endpoint specifier in the connection string, then it works. So guessing you need to either trim slashes from endpoints, or use a URL join function that doesn't add unnecessary slashes to URLs.

issue_title: Idea: Allow searching of hook directory rather than cwd
issue_body:
I don't seem to be able to reference the hook directory when passing in arguments, which makes things like passing in config files stored in the hook repo hard.

It would be nice to be able to do so

similar to `{toxinidir}`

```
arguments: ['-c', '{hookdir}/my-config.js']
```

(would be happy to take this ticket if sounds ok)

issue_title: More algorithms for state sequence decoding
issue_body:
See J. Lember & A. Koloydenko ["Bridging Viterbi and Posterior Decoding: A Generalized
Risk Approach to Hidden Path Inference Based on Hidden Markov Models"][paper].

[paper]: http://www.jmlr.org/papers/volume15/lember14a/lember14a.pdf

Hi, thanks for developing this package! I ran into a problem when trying to download files from [PDB](ftp://ftp.wwpdb.org/pub/pdb/data/structures/divided/pdb/), as it doesn't seem to require a login, and aioftp fails to download. 

My test code looks like this:
```python
import asyncio
import aioftp
import uvloop


async def download_file(session, url):
    url = url.lstrip("ftp://ftp.wwpdb.org")
    id = url[-11:-7]
    ID = id.upper()
    await session.download(url, f"{ID}.ent.gz")
    print(url)


async def unzip(sem, work_queue):
    while not work_queue.empty():
        queue_url = await work_queue.get()
        async with sem:
            async with aioftp.ClientSession("ftp.wwpdb.org") as session:
                await download_file(session, queue_url)
        

def download_queue(urls):
    loop = uvloop.new_event_loop()
    asyncio.set_event_loop(loop)
    q = asyncio.Queue()
    sem = asyncio.Semaphore(10)
    [q.put_nowait(url) for url in urls]
    tasks = [asyncio.ensure_future(unzip(sem, q)) for _ in range(len(urls))]
    loop.run_until_complete(asyncio.gather(*tasks))
    # Zero-sleep to allow underlying connections to close
    loop.run_until_complete(asyncio.sleep(0))
    loop.close()

issue_title: Raise an exception if trying to write into closed response
issue_body:
See https://stackoverflow.com/questions/47175297/handling-premature-client-disconnection-in-aiohttp

The problem is: `await drain()` does nothing because the buffer is not overflown, the buffer is not overflown because sent data are ignored and not accumulated in internal buffer.
`transport.is_closing()` should be used for check.

It should be fixed in payload writer, affects both client and server.

issue_title: Why does MathJax render equations without `$$` in the notebook?
issue_body:
In the "textbooks with jupyter" project, I realized that there was an interesting difference of equation rendering behavior between jupyter notebooks UI and how Jekyll renders the markdown. Specifically, if I put a set of equations inside a markdown cell in Jupyter Notebooks, but without the `$$` delimiting a mathjax block, then it'll still be rendered. E.g., [from this page](https://www.inferentialthinking.com/chapters/14/1/Properties_of_the_Mean)
```
The mean of the collection {2, 3, 3, 9} is 4.25, which is not the "halfway point" of the data. So then what does the mean measure?

To see this, notice that the mean can be calculated in different ways.

\begin{align*}
\mbox{mean} ~ &=~ 4.25 \\ \\
&=~ \frac{2 + 3 + 3 + 9}{4} \\ \\
&=~ 2 \cdot \frac{1}{4} ~~ + ~~ 3 \cdot \frac{1}{4} ~~ + ~~ 3 \cdot \frac{1}{4} ~~ + ~~ 9 \cdot \frac{1}{4} \\ \\
&=~ 2 \cdot \frac{1}{4} ~~ + ~~ 3 \cdot \frac{2}{4} ~~ + ~~ 9 \cdot \frac{1}{4} \\ \\
&=~ 2 \cdot 0.25 ~~ + ~~ 3 \cdot 0.5 ~~ + ~~ 9 \cdot 0.25
\end{align*}

The last expression is an example of a general fact: when we calculate the mean, each distinct value in the collection is *weighted* by the proportion of times it appears in the collection.
```

this will render fine in the notebook, but when Jekyll tries to render it, MathJax won't parse it unless you add `$$`.

I'm wondering if there's some kind of mathjax configuration that the notebook uses to "auto-detect" code blocks like this, or if the notebook app is doing some sanitizing of the markdown before rendering. Anybody have an idea on this? maybe @Carreau or @takluyver ?

issue_title: Question on the implementation of the backward pass
issue_body:
Hi Autograd team, I noticed that there is a `toposort` sort in the backward pass

https://github.com/HIPS/autograd/blob/442205dfefe407beffb33550846434baa90c4de7/autograd/core.py#L17-L24

issue_title: Suggestion drop python 2 support and start using type hints.
issue_body:
Python 3 was released in December 2008. It is now almost ten years later. Anyone who desperately wants to cling to python 2 is welcome to it. But I don't want to miss out on some nice functionality that python3 offers.
One of the things that would be a pleasure to use in ephemeris is type hinting. This allows the IDE to actually know what type of variable you are working with. It prevents a lot of mistakes and makes it easier to develop. Now I often have to look up the original function to check what is being done and see whether this variable is some random class, a list, string or something else. Also some stuff will be checked at compile time, catching errors before they occur. This will be very nice when integrating pytest #80 and refactoring shed-tools #91.
So I suggest we drop the python2 testing and focus our efforts on python3 for the coming releases. 
People who still need a python2 version of ephemeris: the releases up to 0.8.0 are still pretty solid and useful.

issue_title: [OSX] skbuild.setup defaults cmake_args without checking kwargs for the arguments.
issue_body:
OS: OSX/Darwin (testing on travis).

`setup` checks the command line arguments for `-DCMAKE_OSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET` and for `-DCMAKE_OSX_ARCHITECTURES`, and if they don't exist, it defaults them [here](https://github.com/scikit-build/scikit-build/blob/d8e4db57e0a930db75bd204e47b64ba83a7300bb/skbuild/setuptools_wrap.py#L461-L477). The issue with this is that if the user passed either of those arguments to setup via a kwarg. i.e. `setup(..., cmake_args=[..., "-DCMAKE_OSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET:STRING=10.7"])`, it gets overwritten with the default, even when nothing had been passed to the command line. 
This causes builds to break for no apparent reason (see the reasons for [this PR](https://github.com/skvark/opencv-python/pull/110)). A simple fix is moving the lines where the command line arguments are added to the arguments passed via kwarg from [here](https://github.com/scikit-build/scikit-build/blob/d8e4db57e0a930db75bd204e47b64ba83a7300bb/skbuild/setuptools_wrap.py#L503-L506) to right above the block mentioned above. 
Since `cmake_args` is not referenced between line 477 and line 503, nothing will get more args than it expected, and CLI will still override the args passed via kwarg, but default will only be set if neither the CLI or kwarg `cmake_arg` are set.
If this is approved I will PR the fix described above.

DHT storage is currently not durable (i.e., key-value pairs are not stored on disk and a DHT node will lose all data on a reboot). This might be fine when the DHT network becomes large, but for bootstrapping the network we want to ensure that we don't lose data in the DHT e.g., if AWS reboots the VMs that Onename is running.
